title: sesamum calycinum var. angustifolium
intro: Sesamum calycinum var. angustifolium, also known as West African senna or narrow-leafed sesamum, is a plant species within the sesamum family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its anthraquinone content. The health benefits of Sesamum calycinum var. angustifolium include relief from digestive disorders, as well as its potential to act as a laxative and to reduce inflammation. Sesamum calycinum var. angustifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, or infusions for its medicinal uses, although it is recommended to consult a medical professional before using the plant for therapeutic purposes.
